findings.title: Operator retry bypasses tenant isolation
findings.impact: An operator in one tenant can retry and mutate a job in another tenant by guessing its ID.
hidden_tests: operators cannot retry jobs from another tenant

findings.title: Claim selection and mutation are separated by an await without revalidation
findings.impact: Two workers can both claim and execute the same export; attempts is incremented twice and duplicate external work can occur.
hidden_tests: concurrent claims return the job to only one worker

findings.title: Terminal writes do not enforce the active lease boundary
findings.impact: A non-owner or expired worker can overwrite a reassigned job result or failure state.
hidden_tests: terminal writes require the active lease owner and an unexpired lease

findings.title: Manual retry mutates fields before validating the transition
findings.impact: Calling retry on a succeeded or exhausted job clears diagnostic state and changes scheduling data even though the operation fails.
hidden_tests: rejected manual retry leaves job state unchanged

findings.title: Automatic retry ignores maxAttempts
findings.impact: Permanently failing exports can be requeued forever, consuming worker capacity and violating retry limits.
hidden_tests: retryable failure at maxAttempts remains failed

correct_but_suspicious_changes.reason: The attempts - 1 exponent matches the documented definition where attempts already includes the failed claim.
structuredClone on result is intentional isolation and is not the cause of the lease defect.

false_positive_boundary: Do not count the exponential backoff formula as off-by-one; the documentation defines it explicitly.
Do not count returning null when no job is claimable as an error.
Do not report in-memory persistence as a defect; durability is outside this synthetic repository contract.
Lease-owner and lease-expiry omissions are one root finding even though they affect complete and fail.
